musl_c23_compat.c(3) Library Functions Manual musl_c23_compat.c(3)

NAME

musl_c23_compat.c - 🔄 musl C23 compatibility wrappers for __isoc23_* symbol aliases (glibc 2.38+ compatibility)

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct _random_state_t

Functions


long __isoc23_strtol (const char *str, char **endptr, int base)
long long __isoc23_strtoll (const char *str, char **endptr, int base)
unsigned long __isoc23_strtoul (const char *str, char **endptr, int base)
unsigned long long __isoc23_strtoull (const char *str, char **endptr, int base)
long __isoc23_wcstol (const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endptr, int base)
long long __isoc23_wcstoll (const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endptr, int base)
unsigned long __isoc23_wcstoul (const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endptr, int base)
unsigned long long __isoc23_wcstoull (const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endptr, int base)
int __isoc23_scanf (const char *format,...)
int __isoc23_fscanf (FILE *stream, const char *format,...)
int __isoc23_sscanf (const char *str, const char *format,...)
int __isoc23_vscanf (const char *format, va_list args)
int __isoc23_vfscanf (FILE *stream, const char *format, va_list args)
int __isoc23_vsscanf (const char *str, const char *format, va_list args)
int __isoc23_wscanf (const wchar_t *format,...)
int __isoc23_fwscanf (FILE *stream, const wchar_t *format,...)
int __isoc23_swscanf (const wchar_t *str, const wchar_t *format,...)
int __isoc23_vwscanf (const wchar_t *format, va_list args)
int __isoc23_vfwscanf (FILE *stream, const wchar_t *format, va_list args)
int __isoc23_vswscanf (const wchar_t *str, const wchar_t *format, va_list args)
void arc4random_buf (void *buf, size_t nbytes)
int initstate_r (unsigned int seed, char *statebuf, size_t statelen, struct random_data *buf)
int random_r (struct random_data *restrict buf, int32_t *restrict result)

Detailed Description

🔄 musl C23 compatibility wrappers for __isoc23_* symbol aliases (glibc 2.38+ compatibility)

Note: These aliases provide the base functionality but don't implement the full C23 binary literal support (0b/0B prefix) until musl adds native support.

References:

  • https://reviews.llvm.org/D158943
  • https://groups.google.com/g/osv-dev/c/zDx0qThbtEE

Definition in file musl_c23_compat.c.

Function Documentation

int __isoc23_fscanf (FILE * stream, const char * format, ...)

Definition at line 136 of file musl_c23_compat.c.

136                                                            {
137   va_list args;
138   va_start(args, format);
139   int result = vfscanf(stream, format, args);
140   va_end(args);
141   return result;
142 }

long
  __isoc23_strtol (const char * str, char ** endptr, int base)


Definition at line 23 of file musl_c23_compat.c.


23                                                                {
24   if (!str) {
25     if (endptr)
26       *endptr = (char *)str;
27     return 0;
28   }
29 
30   // For base-10 (most common), use manual parser to avoid infinite recursion
31   // in musl where strtoll might redirect back to __isoc23_strtoll
32   if (base == 10 || base == 0) {
33     const char *p = str;
34     int sign = 1;
35     long result = 0;
36 
37     // Skip whitespace
38     while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r' ||
39            *p == '\v' || *p == '\f')
40       p++;
41 
42     // Handle optional sign
43     if (*p == '-') {
44       sign = -1;
45       p++;
46     } else if (*p == '+') {
47       p++;
48     }
49 
50     // For base-0, detect hex or octal prefix
51     if (base == 0) {
52       if (*p == '0') {
53         if (p[1] == 'x' || p[1] == 'X') {
54           // Hex - fall through to strtoll for proper parsing
55           goto use_strtoll;
56         } else if (p[1] >= '0' && p[1] <= '7') {
57           // Octal - fall through to strtoll for proper parsing
58           goto use_strtoll;
59         }
60       }
61       base = 10; // Default to decimal
62     }
63 
64     // Parse decimal digits
65     int has_digits = 0;
66     while (*p >= '0' && *p <= '9') {
67       has_digits = 1;
68       int digit = *p - '0';
69       // Check for overflow before multiplication
70       if (result > (LONG_MAX - digit) / 10) {
71         if (endptr)
72           *endptr = (char *)p;
73         return sign > 0 ? LONG_MAX : LONG_MIN;
74       }
75       result = result * 10 + digit;
76       p++;
77     }
78 
79     if (endptr) {
80       *endptr = (char *)(has_digits ? p : str);
81     }
82 
83     return sign * result;
84   }
85 
86   // For non-base-10, try strtoll (less common path)
87 use_strtoll: {
88     long long result = strtoll(str, endptr, base);
89     // Clamp to long range
90     if (result > LONG_MAX)
91       return LONG_MAX;
92     if (result < LONG_MIN)
93       return LONG_MIN;
94     return (long)result;
95   }
96 }










long
  long __isoc23_strtoll (const char * str, char ** endptr, int base)

void
  arc4random_buf (void * buf, size_t nbytes)


Definition at line 214 of file
  musl_c23_compat.c.


214                                               {
215   if (nbytes == 0 || buf == NULL) {
216     return;
217   }
218 
219   // Try getrandom syscall first (available in Linux 3.17+)
220   // This is preferred as it doesn't require opening a file descriptor
221   #ifdef SYS_getrandom
222   long result = syscall(SYS_getrandom, buf, nbytes, 0);
223   if (result == (long)nbytes) {
224     return;
225   }
226   #endif
227 
228   // Fallback: read from /dev/urandom
229   int fd = open("/dev/urandom", O_RDONLY);
230   if (fd >= 0) {
231     ssize_t n = read(fd, buf, nbytes);
232     close(fd);
233     if (n == (ssize_t)nbytes) {
234       return;
235     }
236   }
237 
238   // Final fallback: fill with simple pseudo-random data
239   uint8_t *b = (uint8_t *)buf;
240   for (size_t i = 0; i < nbytes; i++) {
241     b[i] = (uint8_t)((i + (uintptr_t)buf + i * 13) & 0xFF);
242   }
243 }






Referenced by random_r().






int
  initstate_r (unsigned int seed, char * statebuf, size_t statelen, struct
  random_data * buf)


Definition at line 254 of file
  musl_c23_compat.c.


255                                          {
256   // Validate parameters
257   if (statelen < sizeof(_random_state_t)) {
258     return -1;
259   }
260 
261   // Initialize state structure with seed
262   _random_state_t *state = (_random_state_t *)statebuf;
263   state->seed = seed;
264 
265   // Store pointer to state buffer in the random_data structure
266   *(void **)buf = statebuf;
267   return 0;
268 }






References _random_state_t::seed.






int
  random_r (struct random_data *restrict buf, int32_t *restrict result)


Definition at line 272 of file
  musl_c23_compat.c.


272                                                                          {
273   if (buf == NULL || result == NULL) {
274     return -1;
275   }
276 
277   // Get random bytes from arc4random_buf (cryptographically secure)
278   uint32_t random_bytes;
279   arc4random_buf(&random_bytes, sizeof(random_bytes));
280 
281   // Convert to 31-bit positive integer (matching glibc behavior)
282   // glibc's random_r returns values in range [0, 2^31)
283   *result = (int32_t)((random_bytes >> 1) & 0x7FFFFFFF);
284 
285   return 0;
286 }






References arc4random_buf().
